identity.title: All Other Petroleum and Coal Products Manufacturing

lens.included_activities: Independent lower-middle-market specialty wax, petrolatum, and related refined-petroleum processors that repeatedly provide formulated products and custom processing to external industrial customers.
lens.excluded_activities: Coke-oven operations, petroleum coke, commodity biodiesel blending, packaged fuels and briquettes, captive refinery units, integrated majors, shells, and non-control financing vehicles.
lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Repeat B2B product and custom-processing revenue from formulated waxes and petroleum-derived specialties, sold through purchase orders, supply agreements, formula or non-formula pricing, and customer-specific processing relationships.
lens.deviation_from_default: The residual code combines economically distinct coke, wax, petrolatum, fuel-blending, and briquette activities. It is narrowed to independent specialty wax/petrolatum processors with repeat B2B and custom-processing relationships so one operating and retention model is coherent.

a.bridge: BLS occupation evidence covers the broader refinery-heavy NAICS 324 subsector, while the lens is small specialty wax and custom processors. I map portions of engineering analysis, laboratory documentation, quality review, scheduling, procurement, sales, customer service, and administration to AI-addressable tasks, leaving physical reactors, distillation, forming, packaging, maintenance, logistics, and sampling mostly unexposed. The broader data likely understate technical and customer-facing work in the narrowed lens, biasing the estimate downward.
a.rationale: The lens requires engineers, chemists, laboratories, quality assurance, transportation, process optimization, and customer coordination alongside capital-intensive processing. AI can assist analysis, documentation, planning, and commercial workflows but cannot substitute for most plant and material-handling work.
a.change_evidence: Payroll-weighted occupational rosters and two-week task diaries from at least eight independent specialty processors, linked to current automation and O*NET tasks, would materially change the estimate.
caveats: No six-digit occupation table specific to the narrowed lens was found.
Exposure is judgmentally mapped and excludes tasks already handled by process-control systems.

rho.rationale: Five-year implementation is feasible for document, commercial, planning, and analytic copilots, but specialty processors rely on heterogeneous plant data and safety- and quality-critical workflows. NIST identifies smart-manufacturing advances alongside data-management, integration, explainability, reliability, and high-stakes deployment barriers.
rho.change_evidence: Measured deployments at specialty chemical or wax plants showing hours saved, quality escapes, laboratory cycle time, maintenance outcomes, and required human review would materially move the range.
caveats: No small-firm adoption study specific to NAICS 324199 was available.
The estimate excludes autonomous control of hazardous processes.

e.rationale: The official residual code includes coke-oven products, calcined petroleum coke, waxes, packaged fuels, and other products. Only independent specialty processors with repeat external customer relationships fit the narrowed lens; captive, integrated, commodity, and asset-scale activities are excluded.
e.change_evidence: A firm-by-firm classification of the 13 injected LMM-band firms using plant assets, product mix, ownership, customer type, and custom-processing revenue would directly measure eligibility.
caveats: The detailed Census product mix is from 2002 and is useful for heterogeneity, not current shares.
The small injected count makes the eligible share highly discrete and sensitive to classification.

s5.bridge: Gallup measures five-year ownership intentions for U.S. employer businesses across industries, not completed control transfers among eligible 324199 manufacturers. Its 22% planned sale-or-transfer rate anchors the estimate; I reduce the base for failed processes, family gifts, and internal transfers that do not qualify, while tangible plants and recurring customers support transferability. Direction of net error is uncertain.
s5.rationale: Employer manufacturers can transfer equipment, permits, technical teams, formulas, and customer contracts, but environmental liabilities, feedstock dependence, and plant concentration can deter buyers. Broad survey intentions therefore support a bounded probability, not an observed industry rate.
s5.change_evidence: Five to ten years of closed U.S. specialty wax/petroleum-product deals from PitchBook, Capital IQ, Axial, brokers, and environmental diligence providers, plus owner-age data, would materially change the estimate.
caveats: Survey respondents are much broader and usually smaller than the lens.
Intentions do not equal completed qualifying control transfers.

q.bridge: Trecora is a single, formerly public specialty processor larger than the LMM lens. Its disclosed mix of formula and non-formula pricing, long customer relationships, custom processing, and feedstock-linked pricing shows contract mechanics but not the share of AI savings retained. I infer higher retention for custom and differentiated wax work and lower retention for indexed products; a scaled issuer may have stronger bargaining power, biasing retention upward.
q.rationale: Trecora reported long-term customer relationships, formula pricing tied to prior-month feedstock, and specialized custom manufacturing. Feedstock pass-through does not mechanically pass labor productivity through, and qualification/specialized processing creates friction, but competitive non-formula pricing and renewal negotiations share savings over time.
q.change_evidence: A representative contract and invoice panel separating indexed feedstock adjustments, processing adders, custom-service fees, renewal resets, rebates, and gross-margin changes after productivity improvements would materially change retention.
caveats: One company's disclosures may not represent smaller independent firms.
The source does not isolate AI-derived savings or five-year renewal behavior.

d5.bridge: Trecora's 2022 investor presentation reported historically and prospectively sold-out specialty-wax demand, but this is one issuer's capacity-constrained outlook and not a U.S. constant-quality quantity forecast. I translate it to modest five-year quantity growth for the diversified specialty wax/petrolatum lens, with downside for substitution and cyclical industrial demand. The issuer evidence likely biases growth upward.
d5.rationale: Specialty waxes serve diverse repeat applications in adhesives, coatings, plastics, rubber, inks, textiles, and lubricants. This breadth supports persistence, while petroleum-based feedstock volatility, product substitution, industrial cycles, and sustainability-driven reformulation constrain growth.
d5.change_evidence: A current U.S. volume forecast by application from a specialist market-data provider, reconciled to customer shipment histories and adjusted for quality changes, would materially move the range.
caveats: Demand evidence is company-specific and from 2022.
Sold-out capacity is not the same as end-market quantity growth.

o.rationale: Year-five quantity still requires accountable operators to run hydrogenation, distillation, blending, forming, packaging, laboratories, quality release, and regulated transport. Software can improve selection and coordination but cannot supply the physical material; only a limited share is plausibly eliminated, self-served, or completely replaced by software.
o.change_evidence: Customer interviews and procurement data quantifying in-sourcing, reformulation away from petroleum waxes, toll-processing displacement, and material-intensity reduction by application would materially change the estimate.
caveats: Operator persistence is inferred from process requirements rather than directly measured.
Material substitution could reduce operator-required quantity more than assumed in particular applications.

narrative.executive_view: The coherent opportunity inside this residual code is specialty wax and related custom processing, where repeat applications, technical qualification, and physical processing sustain customer relationships. AI affects indirect and analytic labor more than plant labor, and the total eligible population is probably small.
narrative.ai_impact: AI can assist process-data analysis, laboratory and quality documentation, production scheduling, preventive-maintenance triage, procurement, pricing support, customer inquiries, and regulatory records. Hazardous physical operations, sampling, testing, loading, and accountable release remain human- and asset-dependent.
narrative.value_capture: Custom processing, differentiated wax properties, long customer relationships, and qualification work can protect productivity gains. Formula pricing explicitly moves feedstock costs through, while competitive non-formula business and renewal negotiations still pressure processing margins.
narrative.firm_availability: The code is highly heterogeneous and the injected LMM count is only 13. Eligible specialty processors have transferable plants, permits, know-how, teams, and contracts, but environmental exposure and feedstock concentration complicate transactions.
narrative.demand_durability: Adhesives, coatings, plastics, rubber, inks, textiles, and lubricants provide diverse recurring demand. Substitution, sustainability requirements, industrial cyclicality, and customer reformulation create downside, but software cannot replace the physical service basket.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: Eligibility and current product mix are the largest uncertainties. Site incidents, environmental remediation, sole-source feedstock, logistics interruption, commodity volatility, customer concentration, and obsolete assets can outweigh modest labor savings and sharply reduce transferability.
narrative.principal_driver: Specialized physical processing and qualification support durable repeat B2B and custom-manufacturing relationships.
narrative.principal_weakness: The residual code is heterogeneous and tiny, leaving the eligible firm count and representative economics weakly observed.

sources.title: Trecora Resources 2021 Annual Report
sources.supports: Specialty-wax processes, custom manufacturing, workforce capabilities, customer relationships, pricing mechanics, applications, and operating risks.

sources.supports: Broader-subsector production occupation mix and wages used as an exposure proxy.

sources.supports: Industrial AI applications and barriers involving data, integration, explainability, reliability, and high-stakes operation.

sources.supports: Official six-digit definition and detailed evidence that the code spans coke products, petroleum waxes, and other product classes.

sources.title: Most Small-Business Owners Lack a Succession Plan
sources.supports: Five-year sale or transfer intentions among U.S. employer-business owners.

sources.title: Trecora Resources First Quarter 2022 Earnings Presentation
sources.supports: Contracted revenue, customer tenure, pricing structure, custom-processing economics, and specialty-wax demand evidence.
